---
title: "Uncertainty_Indirect_Leaching_Biosolid_CA"
author: "Dr. Chih-Yu Hung"
date: "2026-04-28"
output: html_document
---

```{r setup}
# --- Packages ----
library(tidyverse)
library(future.apply)

knitr::opts_chunk$set(
  echo = TRUE,
  message = FALSE,
  warning = FALSE
)
```

```{r data_and_function}
# --- Data preparation ----
# Goal: uncertainty of indirect N2O from N leaching due to biosolid N application.
# This analysis keeps only rows with biosolid N application and the selected inventory year.

target_year <- 2015

PPE <- read_csv("Inputs/INPUT-EF_Fixed_Para.csv") # Ecodistrict level P and PE
DF  <- readRDS("Inputs/BiosolidN_corrected.rds")  # Corrected biosolid N from Chang's work

Data <- DF %>%
  left_join(PPE, by = c("Ecodistrict_ID", "Province_ID")) %>%
  select(Inv_Year_ID, Province_ID, Ecodistrict_ID, BION_corrected, P, PE) %>%
  rename(Fert_LCA_kg = BION_corrected) %>%
  filter(
    Inv_Year_ID == target_year,
    Fert_LCA_kg > 0
  ) %>%
  drop_na()


# --- Leaching N2O calculation ----
# Frac_leach follows the IPCC 2019 Refinement style precipitation to evapotranspiration rule.
# EF5 is the indirect N2O emission factor for leached N.
# This function returns province level totals and implied emission factors for each MC draw.

calc_leaching <- function(df, Frac_min, Frac_max, PPE_min, PPE_max, EF5) {
  slope     <- (Frac_max - Frac_min) / (PPE_max - PPE_min)
  intercept <- Frac_min - slope * PPE_min
  eps <- 1e-9
  
  df %>%
    mutate(
      PPE_ratio = P / pmax(PE, eps),
      Frac_leach = case_when(
        PPE_ratio >= PPE_max ~ Frac_max,
        PPE_ratio <= PPE_min ~ Frac_min,
        TRUE ~ slope * PPE_ratio + intercept
      ),
      Leach_factor = Frac_leach * EF5,
      N2O = Fert_LCA_kg * Leach_factor
    ) %>%
    group_by(Province_ID) %>%
    summarise(
      Tot.Fert = sum(Fert_LCA_kg, na.rm = TRUE),
      Tot.N2O  = sum(N2O, na.rm = TRUE),
      N2O.IEF  = if_else(Tot.Fert > 0, Tot.N2O / Tot.Fert, NA_real_),
      .groups = "drop"
    )
}
```

```{r mc_settings}
# --- User knobs ----
# n_mc can be increased after testing the script with a smaller number.
# On Windows, keep workers modest to avoid memory pressure.

use_vec    <- requireNamespace("vctrs", quietly = TRUE)
n_mc       <- 5000
seed       <- 2026
workers    <- 4
batch_size <- 25
set.seed(seed)

# --- Random draw helpers ----
# p95 is the fractional half width of the 95 percent confidence interval.
# For example, p95 = 0.10 means mean plus or minus 10 percent as a 95 percent interval.

rnorm_ci <- function(mu, p95) {
  sd <- abs(p95) * abs(mu) / 1.96
  rnorm(length(mu), mean = mu, sd = sd)
}

# Draws n values from a triangular distribution with minimum a, mode c, and maximum b.
rtri1 <- function(n, a, c, b) {
  stopifnot(a <= c, c <= b, a < b)
  u <- runif(n)
  Fc <- (c - a) / (b - a)
  x <- ifelse(
    u < Fc,
    a + sqrt(u * (b - a) * (c - a)),
    b - sqrt((1 - u) * (b - a) * (b - c))
  )
  pmax(a, pmin(x, b))
}
```

```{r mc_worker}
# --- Core per iteration worker ----
# Each iteration perturbs activity data and model parameters, then recalculates leaching N2O.
# Biosolid N uncertainty combines biosolid amount uncertainty and N content uncertainty by error propagation.

run_one_mc <- function(i, Data,
                       ci_list = list(
                         Fert_LCA_kg = sqrt(0.15^2 + 0.46^2),
                         P           = 0.10,
                         PE          = 0.10
                       ),
                       par_means = list(
                         Frac_max = 0.30,
                         Frac_min = 0.05,
                         PPE_max  = 1.00,
                         PPE_min  = 0.23,
                         EF5_min  = 0.000,
                         EF5_mode = 0.011,
                         EF5_max  = 0.020
                       ),
                       par_ci = list(
                         Frac_max = 0.15,
                         Frac_min = 0.15
                       )) {
  df <- Data
  message("iteration = ", i)
  
  # --- 1) Stochastic perturbation of input data ----
  df <- df %>%
    mutate(
      Fert_LCA_kg = pmax(rnorm_ci(Fert_LCA_kg, ci_list$Fert_LCA_kg), 0),
      P           = pmax(rnorm_ci(P, ci_list$P), 0),
      PE          = pmax(rnorm_ci(PE, ci_list$PE), 0)
    )
  
  # --- 2) Stochastic perturbation of leaching fraction limits ----
  fmin <- pmin(pmax(rnorm_ci(par_means$Frac_min, par_ci$Frac_min), 0), 1)
  fmax <- pmin(pmax(rnorm_ci(par_means$Frac_max, par_ci$Frac_max), 0), 1)
  
  if (fmax <= fmin) {
    tmp <- fmin
    fmin <- fmax
    fmax <- tmp
  }
  
  # --- 3) Stochastic draw of EF5 ----
  # EF5 is sampled once per MC iteration and applied to all ecodistricts.
  EF5 <- rtri1(
    1,
    a = par_means$EF5_min,
    c = par_means$EF5_mode,
    b = par_means$EF5_max
  )
  
  # --- 4) Run leaching calculation ----
  Result_Prov_MC <- calc_leaching(
    df,
    Frac_min = fmin,
    Frac_max = fmax,
    PPE_min  = par_means$PPE_min,
    PPE_max  = par_means$PPE_max,
    EF5      = EF5
  ) %>%
    mutate(
      MC = i,
      Frac_min_draw = fmin,
      Frac_max_draw = fmax,
      EF5_draw = EF5
    )
  
  Result_Prov_MC
}

# --- Batch worker ----
# Data is serialized once per batch instead of once per MC iteration.

run_batch <- function(ii, Data) {
  acc <- NULL
  
  for (i in ii) {
    res_i <- run_one_mc(i, Data = Data)
    acc <- if (is.null(acc)) res_i else {
      if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(acc, res_i) else bind_rows(acc, res_i)
    }
    rm(res_i)
  }
  
  gc(FALSE)
  acc
}
```

```{r run_mc}
# --- Run Monte Carlo simulation ----
# The result is province level leaching N2O for each MC iteration.

future::plan(future::multisession, workers = workers)
# options(future.globals.maxSize = 2 * 1024^3) # Uncomment if the globals are too large.

idx <- seq_len(n_mc)
batches <- split(idx, ceiling(seq_along(idx) / batch_size))

syst_s <- Sys.time()

parts <- future_lapply(
  batches,
  run_batch,
  Data = Data,
  future.seed = TRUE
)

syst_f <- Sys.time()
syst_f - syst_s

Result_Prov_MC <- if (use_vec) vctrs::vec_rbind(!!!parts) else bind_rows(parts)
rm(parts)
gc(FALSE)

summary(Result_Prov_MC) #2170.12, 4095.5
#saveRDS(Result_Prov_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Leaching_Biosolid_Province_CA.rds")
```

```{r national_summary}
# --- National uncertainty summary ----
# N2O is converted to CO2e using molecular weight conversion and GWP100 = 265.

Result_MC <- Result_Prov_MC %>%
  group_by(MC) %>%
  summarise(
    N2O = sum(Tot.N2O, na.rm = TRUE),
    Tot.Fert = sum(Tot.Fert, na.rm = TRUE),
    N2O.IEF = if_else(Tot.Fert > 0, N2O / Tot.Fert, NA_real_),
    EF5_draw = first(EF5_draw),
    Frac_min_draw = first(Frac_min_draw),
    Frac_max_draw = first(Frac_max_draw),
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  mutate(
    CO2e = N2O * 44 / 28 * 265,
    `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6
  )

summary(Result_MC) #median 15.4541. mean 15.3494
#saveRDS(Result_MC, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Leaching_Biosolid_National_CA.rds")

CI <- Result_MC %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    mean   = mean(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_lower = (lower - median) / median * 100,
    rel_upper = (upper - median) / median * 100
  )

CI #+-76%
```

```{r province_summary}
# --- Province level uncertainty summary ----
# This table shows the median and 95 percent uncertainty interval by province.

CI_Prov <- Result_Prov_MC %>%
  mutate(
    CO2e = Tot.N2O * 44 / 28 * 265,
    `CO2e(kt)` = CO2e / 10^6
  ) %>%
  group_by(Province_ID) %>%
  summarise(
    median = median(`CO2e(kt)`, na.rm = TRUE),
    lower  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.025, na.rm = TRUE),
    upper  = quantile(`CO2e(kt)`, 0.975, na.rm = TRUE),
    rel_lower = (lower - median) / median * 100,
    rel_upper = (upper - median) / median * 100,
    .groups = "drop"
  ) %>%
  arrange(desc(median))

CI_Prov # QC is the most -78 ~ +85%
#saveRDS(CI_Prov, "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Leaching_Biosolid_Province_CI_CA.rds")
```

```{r plot_distribution}
# --- Plot national uncertainty distribution ----
# The dashed vertical line is the median national indirect leaching emission estimate.

p1 <- ggplot(Result_MC, aes(x = `CO2e(kt)`)) +
  geom_histogram(bins = 100, color = "white") +
  geom_vline(xintercept = CI$median, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    x = expression(N[2]*O~CO[2]*e~"(kt)"),
    y = "Monte Carlo count",
    title = "Uncertainty of indirect N2O from biosolid N leaching in Canada"
  ) +
  theme_bw()

p1

ggsave(
  "Outputs/Uncertainty_Indirect_Leaching_Biosolid_CA.png",
  p1,
  width = 7,
  height = 5,
  dpi = 300
)
```
